research_question: Can we keep the calibrated v7 operational contract fixed, but replace the weak single-signal momentum score family with one directionally aligned trend-quality price/volume family, so that the resulting cross-sectional signal has thicker gross edge and better fixed-test robustness?

feature_set.interpretation: lower recent volatility is better for trend quality
higher recent traded amount is better
more consistent recent positive trend is better

score_family_contract.score_rule: mean(percentile_rank(momentum_60_5_raw DESC), percentile_rank(volatility_20d_raw ASC), percentile_rank(liquidity_20d_raw DESC), percentile_rank(trend_consistency_20d_raw DESC)); require min_feature_count >= 3
score_family_contract.missing_data_rule: Only score rows with at least 3 non-null ranked features; otherwise emit null model_score_D0 and preserve the row for audit.

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same calibrated v7 0.70 ranking liquidity guard and same TopK=10 width
constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: same equal-weight daily Top10 extraction used by calibrated v7

disallowed_changes: Do not change the calibrated v7 liquidity guard threshold or guard stage.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not combine this round with weight-penalty, extraction, refresh-rule, or turnover-suppression changes.
Do not sweep multiple trend-quality price-volume feature sets inside this round.
Do not sweep multiple score aggregation formulas inside this round.
Do not reintroduce short-term reversal, PIT quality, value, or investment features into this family scaffold round.
notes: This round changes exactly one core dimension: the score family.
The purpose is to keep the research focus on the mature price-volume data layer while removing the internal directional conflict present in the prior v13 multisignal family.
This registration freezes the family definition and governance boundary first; it does not yet imply implementation or full-chain execution.
